title: Uber layoffs: AI prompts cuts to customer service jobs
excerpt: Uber is laying off 10% of its community operations team, citing AI. Remote workers must relocate to an office, impacting customer service roles.
content_html:
Uber lays off 10% of customer service workers and calls others back to working in the office, citing AI

By Alex Bitter

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Uber is cutting customer service roles — and it says artificial intelligence is the reason.

The ride-hailing company is laying off about 10% of its community operations team and asking remote employees to relocate to a city with an Uber office and work there three days a week, it said in a statement on Thursday. Bloomberg earlier reported the news.

AI presents "a massive opportunity for us to accelerate output, improve quality, and scale customer solutions at pace," Megha Yethadka, Uber's vice president of global community operations, wrote in a memo to the team on Wednesday.

"We've made some strides, but to unlock this potential, we need an effective organization to layer AI on," Yethadka wrote. "We cannot scale frontier technology on top of fragmented processes."

An Uber spokesperson said the company informed the team on Wednesday about the "structural changes we are making to simplify operations, strengthen in-person collaboration, and continue to embrace AI."

The layoffs add Uber to the list of companies that have cut jobs in the name of AI. Others this year include Block, IBM, and Snap.

Last month, Uber laid off a quarter of its human resources and recruitment staff. The cuts affected less than 1% of the company's 34,000-person global workforce and weren't connected to AI, Uber said at the time.

CEO Dara Khosrowshahi has said that Uber is slowing down hiring as it invests more in AI.

Have a tip? Contact this reporter at abitter@businessinsider.com or via encrypted messaging app Signal at 808-854-4501. Use a personal email address, a nonwork WiFi network, and a nonwork device; here's our guide to sharing information securely.

Read next

Alex Bitter

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Alex Bitter is a senior retail reporter covering the gig economy, food, and retail. His work focuses major gig delivery and ride-hailing apps, including Uber, Lyft, DoorDash, Instacart, and Walmart's Spark. He is interested in everything from what it's like to work on the apps to the companies' business strategies.Some of his recent stories feature gig workers who have been deactivated on the apps, DoorDash hiring traditional employees to make deliveries, gig workers' use of bots, and gig work expanding into new professions, such as nursing.Alex has also written about Aldi's US expansion, Starbucks' turnaround efforts, and the fallout from Kraft-Heinz's budget cutting. Convenience store chain Sheetz ended its "smile policy" after his reporting.Before joining Insider in September 2020, he wrote about consumer and retail companies for S&P Global Market Intelligence. He's a graduate of the University of Hawai'i at Mānoa and grew up on the Big Island.Alex lives in the Washington, DC, area, where you can find him studying ancient coins or searching for Civil War artifacts with his metal detector in his free time.Got a tip? Reach out at abitter@businessinsider.com or via encrypted messaging app Signal at +1 (808) 854-4501.

content_html_zh:
Uber 援引人工智能解雇了 10% 的客户服务人员，并让其他人回到办公室工作

作者：Alex Bitter 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

优步正在削减客户服务职位，并表示人工智能就是原因。

这家叫车公司在周四的一份声明中表示，将裁员约 10% 的社区运营团队，并要求远程员工搬到设有 Uber 办事处的城市，每周工作三天。 彭博社早些时候报道了这一消息。

Uber 全球社区运营副总裁梅加·耶塔德卡 (Megha Yethadka) 在周三给团队的一份备忘录中写道，人工智能“为我们提供了巨大的机会，可以加速产出、提高质量并快速扩展客户解决方案”。

Yethadka 写道：“我们已经取得了一些进展，但为了释放这一潜力，我们需要一个有效的组织来分层人工智能。” “我们无法在分散的流程之上扩展前沿技术。”

Uber 发言人表示，该公司周三向团队通报了“我们正在进行的结构性变革，以简化运营、加强面对面协作并继续拥抱人工智能”。

此次裁员使 Uber 跻身以人工智能名义裁员的公司名单中。 今年的其他公司包括 Block、IBM 和 Snap。

上个月，Uber 解雇了四分之一的人力资源和招聘人员。 Uber 当时表示，此次裁员影响到该公司 34,000 名全球员工中不到 1%，且与人工智能无关。

首席执行官达拉·科斯罗萨西 (Dara Khosrowshahi) 表示，优步正在放慢招聘速度，因为它加大了对人工智能的投资。

有小费吗？ 请通过 abitter@businessinsider.com 或通过加密消息应用程序 Signal 联系本记者，电话：808-854-4501。 使用个人电子邮件地址、非工作 WiFi 网络和非工作设备；这是我们安全共享信息的指南。

Alex Bitter 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

亚历克斯·比特（Alex Bitter）是一位资深零售记者，报道零工经济、食品和零售业。 他的工作重点是主要的零工配送和叫车应用程序，包括 Uber、Lyft、DoorDash、Instacart 和沃尔玛的 Spark。 他对从应用程序上工作的感觉到公司的业务战略等一切都很感兴趣。他最近的一些故事讲述了在应用程序上被停用的零工工人、DoorDash 雇用传统员工进行送货、零工工人使用机器人以及零工工作扩展到护理等新职业。亚历克斯还撰写了有关阿尔迪在美国的扩张、星巴克的扭亏为盈以及卡夫亨氏预算削减的影响的文章。 便利店连锁店 Sheetz 在他的报道后结束了“微笑政策”。在 2020 年 9 月加入 Insider 之前，他为标准普尔全球市场情报撰写了有关消费者和零售公司的文章。 他毕业于夏威夷大学马诺阿分校，在大岛长大。亚历克斯住在华盛顿特区，你可以发现他在空闲时间研究古钱币或用金属探测器寻找内战文物。有提示吗？ 请通过 abitter@businessinsider.com 或通过加密消息应用程序 Signal 联系，电话 +1 (808) 854-4501。
